prompt:
My kid's classroom kiosk needs a two-player concentration (memory) game and I'm supplying the logic layer. The tablet app is React, so the engine must be pure state-in state-out — plain objects, no classes, no mutation, definitely no shuffling (the layouts are authored by the teacher as fixtures). Build memory.ts (stdlib only, no npm); test_memory.ts is the acceptance suite and stays as shipped.

newGame(layout) takes a fixture layout string: rectangular lines of uppercase letters A-Z where every letter used appears EXACTLY twice (a trailing newline is allowed). Reject empty or ragged layouts, any non-uppercase character, and any symbol count other than two — the error messages should say what's wrong ('ragged', 'bad card symbol', how many times a symbol 'appears'). The state is plain data: rows, cols, cards, matched (booleans), up (the [r,c] of the first card of the pair in progress, else null), current (player 1 or 2, P1 starts), scores ([p1, p2] pairs banked), pairsDone (how many two-card attempts have been completed).

flip(game, r, c) returns a NEW state and never touches its argument. Out-of-bounds throws RangeError; flipping on a finished game, flipping an already-matched card, or flipping the very card that's already face up this turn all throw Error. First flip of a turn just goes face up. Second flip completes the attempt: equal symbols means both cards lock as matched, the current player's score goes up by one and — house rule, the kids insist — THE SAME PLAYER GOES AGAIN; different symbols means both go back face down and the turn passes. Either way pairsDone increments. isOver(game) is true when everything is matched; winner(game) is 1, 2, 'tie', or null while play continues.

render(game) is the table as the players see it: '#' for face-down, the face-up pending card as its uppercase letter, matched pairs as LOWERCASE letters; rows joined with \n, no trailing newline. peek(game) is for the test harness: every face visible, matched lowercase, same shape.

transcript(layout, flips) replays a scripted list of [r, c] flips and narrates: each flip is a line 'P<n> flips (r,c)=S'; when a pair completes, either 'P<n> match S -> <their new score>' or 'no match -> P<other>'; when the last pair falls, close with 'final P1=<a> P2=<b>' then 'winner: P<n>' (or 'winner: tie') and stop even if more flips remain. A script may end mid-game — then there are simply no closing lines. Illegal flips inside a script propagate as the same errors flip throws.

node --test test_memory.ts must pass as shipped.
